title: carlina vulgaris subsp. vulgaris
intro: Carlina vulgaris subsp. vulgaris, also known as the carline thistle or carline, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the sunflower family, characterized by its tall stature, prickly stems, and purple flowers. The medicinal properties of Carlina vulgaris subsp. vulgaris include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate digestive problems, and support the immune system, making it a valuable natural remedy for various ailments. Carlina vulgaris subsp. vulgaris is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by infusing the flowers and leaves in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
